justification: All pass criteria met: (1) SLA math clearly identified — 99.9% Onfido dependency makes 99.95% team SLA unachievable, with explicit calculation of ~44 min vs ~22 min downtime/month. (2) Multiple mitigation options recommended — async KYC, circuit breaker + fallback, multi-provider failover, and accepting lower SLA. (3) Circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as 'single biggest operational gap' with detailed cascade failure explanation. (4) Health endpoint / Onfido coupling issue identified with specific recommendation to separate liveness from readiness checks. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with escalation to architect (MJ) for SLA decision.

observations.text: SLA math is front-and-center with clear numerical breakdown and the key insight that effective uptime is multiplicative
Comprehensive failure mode table covering six distinct scenarios with current handling and recommendations
Health endpoint issue correctly identified with specific fix: separate liveness from readiness probes
Circuit breaker gap identified with concrete cascade failure mechanism (connection pool exhaustion)
Recommendations are prioritized into must-haves, high-priority, and nice-to-haves — actionable and realistic
Catches additional issues beyond rubric: missing retry logic, idempotency concerns, rate-limit handling, observability gaps in error-type breakdown
Escalation table correctly routes SLA decision to architect rather than making the business call itself
